title: erigeron vetensis
intro: Erigeron vetensis, commonly known as Mexican daisy or frostweed, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Erigeron vetensis include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Erigeron vetensis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for topical application, and is also used in some herbal remedies for its antiseptic and wound-healing properties.
